name: AEDC
description: African Investor. AEDC develops, builds and operates renewable energy projects in East Africa. Our team has many years of experience including development of over 1,250 MW of now operating wind and solar projects in North America. AEDC completed development and financing of the Malindi Solar project, East Africa?s first large solar project. This is a 52 MWp solar PV project (total investment of USD $69M) set on 450 acres west of Malindi, Kenya. AEDC continues to develop large sites in Kenya. AEDC helps commercial and industrial customers in Kenya by offering a cheaper and more reliable source of power and allowing customers to better manage and control their biggest business expense. We are a complete solution provider, offering our customers fully financed options to best suit their business operations, including solar, other renewable technologies, storage and efficiency retrofits.

tavily.answer:
The official website for AEDC is https://www.arnold.af.mil. This site provides information on the Arnold Air Force Base and its test capabilities. For other regional AEDC entities, visit https://www.arkansasedc.com, https://aedcweb.com, https://www.abujaelectricity.com, and https://www.adirondackedc.org.
American Engineering & Development Corporation (AEDC) is a civil engineering contractor based in South Florida. Adirondack Economic Development Corporation (AEDC) is a non-profit economic development organization in northern New York. Anchorage Economic Development Corporation (AEDC) focuses on economic development in Anchorage, Alaska.
AEDC is a non-profit CDFI aiding small businesses in Northern New York. Crunchbase is a startup database service that spun out from AOL in 2015. Crunchbase lists over 1.8 million companies and 6.2 million funding records.
Wellfound is a job board for tech startups, offering direct connections and AI-powered recruiting tools. It's legitimate and widely used, focusing on mid-to-senior tech roles.
Dealroom is a global data platform for startups and tech ecosystems, launched in 2013. It raised $7 million to expand its mapping of tech ecosystems. The platform provides data-driven insights for investors and policymakers.
